title: Aspect in future and imperatives
subtitle: Commands, requests, and talking about what you will do

vocabulary.meaning: I will be / you will be / he/she will be
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: I will be reading / I will read.

vocabulary.meaning: tell me / say (imperative of сказать, informal/formal)
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: Tell me please, where is the station?

hook.text: Two topics remain for aspect: future tense and commands. The future is where you'll finally see perfective conjugations in use. Commands (imperatives) need aspect too — 'read it!' vs 'read!' are different in Russian. Once you understand this, you have the complete aspect system.

why.text: The imperfective future uses быть (to be) as an auxiliary verb: я буду читать (I will be reading / I will read habitually). The perfective future conjugates like a present tense verb but means future: я прочитаю (I will read it through, I will finish reading). Aspect in commands: imperfective = ongoing/general request; perfective = specific one-time action.

cards.title: Imperfective future — буду + infinitive
cards.body: The imperfective future is formed with the future forms of быть + the imperfective infinitive. It expresses future habit, future process, or ongoing future activity. Use when the action is general or repeated.

examples.meaning: I will read (habitually) / I will be reading

cards.title: Perfective future — conjugated like present
cards.body: The perfective future uses the same personal endings as the present tense, but conjugated from the perfective stem. It expresses a single completed future action — 'I will finish reading', 'I will write (and be done)'. This is why perfective verbs have no present: their 'present-looking' form IS the future.

examples.meaning: I will read it through (finish reading)

cards.title: Imperative — commands and requests
cards.body: To form the imperative: if the stem ends in a vowel, add -й (informal) / -йте (formal/plural). If it ends in a consonant, add -и / -ите. Aspect matters: imperfective imperative = ongoing/general; perfective imperative = one-time specific action.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I will be reading' (habitual/process, future)?
questions.explanation: Imperfective future: буду + infinitive. Я буду читать.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I will finish writing' (completed future)?
questions.explanation: Perfective future: conjugate the perfective verb (написать → я напишу).

questions.prompt: Which imperative asks someone to read the whole text (completed)? читай or прочитай?
questions.explanation: прочитай (perfective) = read it all the way through. читай (imperfective) = read (as an ongoing activity).

cards.title: Negated imperatives — imperfective only
cards.body: Negated commands (don't do X) almost always use the imperfective aspect. не + perfective imperative sounds unnatural. не читай (don't read) not не прочитай. This is a consistent rule.

cards.title: скоро (soon) with perfective future
cards.body: скоро (soon) pairs naturally with perfective future — it implies a single upcoming completed event. Contrast with буду + infinitive for ongoing or uncertain future.

examples.meaning: I will come soon. (one arrival, certain)
I will finish reading the book soon.

questions.prompt: She will be working (future, imperfective): Она ___ работать.
questions.explanation: Imperfective future: she = она → будет. Она будет работать.

questions.prompt: I will finish doing it: Я ___ это.
questions.explanation: Completed single future action → perfective future. сделаю.

questions.prompt: Don't read this (negated command): Не ___ это.
questions.explanation: Negated commands use imperfective. Не читай это.
сказать → imperative formal = скажите. Скажите, пожалуйста.

questions.prompt: We will study (imperfective future): Мы ___ учить.
questions.explanation: Imperfective future: we = мы → будем. Мы будем учить.

rules: Perfective future: conjugate the perfective verb (same endings as present tense)
Imperative: imperfective = ongoing/general command; perfective = one-time specific action
Negated imperatives: always use imperfective (не читай, не делай, не говори)
скоро (soon) naturally pairs with perfective future
summary.nextLesson: Next: Unit 10 — Everyday situations. Ordering food, asking for directions, and practical survival Russian.

questions.prompt: She will be reading (imperfective future): Она ___ читать.
I will finish writing it (perfective future): Я ___.
Don't speak like that (negated command): Не ___ так.
Read it all (perfective command, formal): ___ текст.
I will come soon: Я скоро ___.
